name: Stamina
description: A regenerating exertion pool limits repeated movement, attacks, blocks, climbs, or survival actions.
design_notes: Stamina is an action gate, so every stamina_cost_event_id needs a clear purpose and recovery rule. Designers should decide which actions spend stamina, how stamina_regen_delay_ms interacts with input buffering, and what exhaustion_state_id prevents without making the player feel arbitrarily locked out.
design_purpose: Limit repeated high-output actions such as sprinting, climbing, blocking, dodging, or harvesting.
Create tactical pacing by asking players to alternate exertion and recovery.
Connect hunger, thirst, temperature, equipment load, and injury into moment-to-moment survival decisions.
player_fantasy: The player feels capable but mortal, managing bursts of effort instead of acting without limits.

parameters.name: stamina_capacity_points
parameters.description: Maximum exertion pool available before modifiers from hunger, equipment, injury, or upgrades.

parameters.name: stamina_cost_event_id
parameters.description: Action event that spends stamina and can be audited for balance or networking.

parameters.name: stamina_regen_delay_ms
parameters.description: Delay after the last stamina spend before recovery begins.

parameters.name: regen_points_per_second
parameters.description: Recovery pace while no blocking exhaustion, injury, or activity modifier is active.

parameters.name: exhaustion_state_id
parameters.description: State applied when stamina falls below the minimum threshold for major actions.

parameters.name: minimum_action_stamina_points
parameters.description: Required pool level before a stamina-spending action can begin.

edge_cases: A buffered dodge, attack, or jump becomes valid on the same frame stamina recovers above the action threshold.
Sprinting spends stamina while climbing, swimming, or carrying weight also applies modifiers.
A server correction rewinds stamina after the client already played an accepted action animation.
Stamina reaches zero while the player is over a pit, underwater, or midway through a ladder transition.
common_bugs: Regeneration starts immediately after a spend because stamina_regen_delay_ms is checked against render frames.
Failed actions still spend stamina when validation order is input, cost, then collision or state rejection.
Exhaustion state never clears after save/load because last spend tick or regen lockout was not persisted.
UI shows enough stamina due to prediction but the server rejects the action and leaves input feeling dropped.
balancing_notes: Low stamina capacity makes route planning tense, but too much restriction can make basic traversal feel punitive.
Separate combat and traversal costs if one loop is starving the other of player agency.
Exhaustion should usually reduce options before fully removing control.
accessibility_notes: Provide optional stamina assist, wider input buffering, or slower depletion for players who cannot sustain rapid inputs.
Use clear UI and audio states for exhaustion without relying only on heavy camera sway or breathing effects.

implementation_notes.unity: Keep stamina in a stat component with spend transactions keyed by stamina_cost_event_id. Character and combat controllers should request spends before starting actions, then update UI from predicted values while server authority can correct capacity, spend, and regen delay.
implementation_notes.godot: Drive stamina recovery from a fixed gameplay tick in an autoload survival service or player component. Input buffers should query minimum_action_stamina_points during action resolution, and exhaustion_state_id should be saved with current pool and last spend tick.
implementation_notes.unreal: A replicated attribute set or character component can own stamina capacity, spends, and regen delay. Gameplay Ability activation should commit stamina on the server after validation, while client prediction displays pending spends and correction feedback.
implementation_notes.web: Model stamina as simulation state with explicit spend events and recovery ticks. Phaser or custom controllers should avoid mutating stamina from animation callbacks alone; replay and rollback need spend event ids plus current pool.

example_games.title: Dark Souls
example_games.note: Uses stamina to pace attacks, blocking, dodging, and recovery.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
example_games.note: Uses stamina for climbing, sprinting, gliding, and swimming limits.
